content: When worldwriter and charwriter agents produce conflicting NPC names, charwriter names are canonical. The fixer agent reconciles settlement files to match charwriter output.

Meta files (CHANGELOG, Timeline, World Overview) are updated after each major content session. CHANGELOG uses exact header format '## YYYY-MM-DD — [Settlement Name] Founded'. Timeline uses relative year notation (Year 0 = present, negative for past). World Overview captures emerging themes as headings with explanatory paragraphs.

Caltworks is the founding settlement. Salt Flats region. Key NPCs: Maren Seedeck (Salt Warden), Oran Calt (patriarch), Dessa Vael (hauler organizer). Core tension: labor vs ownership, informal governance under strain, degrading salt flats.

Rackby is Caltworks' primary salt buyer: a ~350-person fish-curing settlement on Greymere lake, 3 days northeast of Caltworks. Salt supply runs through merchant factor Vess Cordham. The connection is economically critical but indirect.

Vess Cordham is the merchant factor connecting Caltworks and Rackby. He controls both the salt procurement and the fish export chain. He knows about Caltworks' salt degradation and has not disclosed it. Canonically: knows more than either settlement.

The Fork is the waypoint staging yard on the salt road between Caltworks and the fishing coast, ~1-1.5 days travel from Caltworks. ~30 permanent residents. Neutral trade ground where factors and haulers negotiate, outside Caltworks' weighmaster oversight.
Vess Cordham is based at Rackby (Factor's Store). He operates the Caltworks-Rackby salt/fish trade route, controls both ends, and is tagged region/greymere in his NPC file.

The Caltworks-to-Rackby salt trade chain is mediated entirely by Vess Cordham: neither settlement has direct contact with the other. The Fork is the staging point where crates change hands. This single-factor dependency is the central economic tension in the region.

Salt trade chain established: Caltworks → The Fork (waypoint) → Rackby (fish-curing on Greymere lake). Vess Cordham is the sole factor controlling this chain. He is the same person as the unnamed merchant factor in earlier Dessa Vael and Maren Seedeck content.
Rackby is the primary salt buyer, a fish-curing settlement of ~350 on Greymere lake. The Fork is a waypoint staging yard (~30 permanent) at the road branch between Caltworks and the coast.

Silthen is the secondary salt supplier Vess Cordham is secretly negotiating with: an estuary reed-cutting settlement (~70 people, 3-4 days west of Caltworks) with tidal pans worked by the Voel family. Hava Denn manages reed plots and burn schedule and is quietly opposed to salt expansion.

Silthen is a reed-cutting estuary settlement 3-4 days west of Caltworks. Grey salt is a byproduct of seasonal reed-burning on brine-saturated silt flats, not deliberately produced. Vess Cordham secretly buys it as a replacement supply for degrading Caltworks salt.

Hava Denn is Silthen's communal reed plot manager and Cordham's contact for grey salt purchases. She manages shared plots on behalf of the settlement collective. She suspects the arrangement may create dependency but has not confronted Cordham.

Silthen integration complete: Cordham's secondary supplier is named explicitly as Silthen; Hava Denn is the grey salt contact; Pell Voel is the tidal pan operator and direct negotiation contact. Cordham's Silthen visit is now dated ~4 months before present (Year -0.33).

Silthen is the western salt supplier Cordham is negotiating with: estuary reed-cutting settlement ~70 people, 3-4 days west of Caltworks on the Silt Run. Grey salt is a byproduct of seasonal reed-burning on brine-saturated silt. Key contacts: Hava Denn (reed plot manager, burn-ash salt) and Pell Voel (tidal pan operator, scalable salt source).
Tallin Oss is The Fork's storage yard operator: keeps 8 seasons of crate logs, knows real vs declared salt output, received Cordham's hold-log request and has not yet complied.

Cedd Miralle is a factor at The Fork: relocated from Caltworks 3 years ago after Maren Seedeck blocked a transaction; has private dataset comparing freight logs to official Caltworks output figures; found ~15-20% discrepancy but does not know about salt degradation.

The Fork: Tallin Oss made a precautionary copy of the discrepancy entries (seasons where Fork incoming weights diverge from Cordham's Rackby receipts) and stored it at the rest house locked chest, separate from the main storage shed logs. Triggered by Cordham's hold-all-logs message.

Cedd Miralle's approach at The Fork: he does not report proactively but will approach any outside investigator who arrives for the right reason. He has a comparison of Fork crate logs vs Caltworks declared output obtained through a weighmaster contact. His motive (Calt family grudge) colors his interpretation but his evidence is real.
